perturbation.original_text: Each of the Stockholders severally agrees that for a period (the "Restrictive Period") which shall commence on the Effective Date of the Merger and shall expire on a date which shall be the latest to occur of: (a) such date as such Stockholder shall no longer be employed on a full-time basis with AUGI or any other subsidiary or affiliate of AUGI; or (b) three (3) years following the effectiveness of the Merger (the "Restrictive Period"), without the prior written consent of AUGI, such Stockholder shall not invest, carry on, engage or become involved, either as an employee, agent, advisor, officer, director, stockholder, manager, partner, joint venturer, participant or consultant in any business enterprise (other than Mergerco, AUGI or any of their respective subsidiaries, affiliates, successors or assigns) which derives any material revenues from the offer or sale in the United States from time to time during the Restrictive Period of services relating to all aspects of providing site acquisition, zoning, architectural and engineering consulting and design services to the wireless telecommunications industry (the "Business"), or which engages in any other business substantially similar to and directly competitive with the Business conducted by the Company as at the date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of the Stockholders severally agrees that for a period (the "Restrictive Period") which shall commence on the Effective Date of the Merger and shall expire on a date which shall be the latest to occur of: (a) such date as such Stockholder shall no longer be employed on a full-time basis with AUGI or any other subsidiary or affiliate of AUGI; or (b) three (3) years following the effectiveness of the Merger (the "Restrictive Period"), without the prior written consent of AUGI, such Stockholder may invest, carry on, engage or become involved, either as an employee, agent, advisor, officer, director, stockholder, manager, partner, joint venturer, participant or consultant in any business enterprise (other than Mergerco, AUGI or any of their respective subsidiaries, affiliates, successors or assigns) which derives any material revenues from the offer or sale in the United States from time to time during the Restrictive Period of services relating to all aspects of providing site acquisition, zoning, architectural and engineering consulting and design services to the wireless telecommunications industry (the "Business"), or which engages in any other business substantially similar to and directly competitive with the Business conducted by the Company as at the date hereof.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall not' to 'may' the clause now contradicts itself. Originally, the Stockholders were restricted from engaging in competitive activities without AUGI's consent. Now, by stating they 'may' engage in such activities, it implies permission, thereby negating the non-compete agreement and introducing an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Competition. Each of the Stockholders severally agrees that for a period (the "Restrictive Period") which shall commence on the Effective Date of the Merger and shall expire on a date which shall be the latest to occur of: (a) such date as such Stockholder shall no longer be employed on a full-time basis with AUGI or any other subsidiary or affiliate of AUGI; or (b) three (3) years following the effectiveness of the Merger (the "Restrictive Period"), without the prior written consent of AUGI, such Stockholder may invest, carry on, engage or become involved, either as an employee, agent, advisor, officer, director, stockholder, manager, partner, joint venturer, participant or consultant in any business enterprise (other than Mergerco, AUGI or any of their respective subsidiaries, affiliates, successors or assigns) which derives any material revenues from the offer or sale in the United States from time to time during the Restrictive Period of services relating to all aspects of providing site acquisition, zoning, architectural and engineering consulting and design services to the wireless telecommunications industry (the "Business"), or which engages in any other business substantially similar to and directly competitive with the Business conducted by the Company as at the date hereof. Nothing contained in this Section 1 shall be deemed to prevent a Stockholder from (a) leasing or renting real property in which the Stockholder owns a direct or <PAGE> indirect interest to a person or entity engaged in the Business; or (b) beneficially owning, directly or indirectly, 5% or less of any class of securities of an entity that has a class of securities registered under Section 12 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, so long as such securities ownership does not include any operational, managerial or consulting relationship with such corporation or entity.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the Stockholders 'shall not' engage in certain business activities without AUGI's consent, establishing a clear non-compete restriction. The changed text replaces 'shall not' with 'may', directly contradicting the initial restriction by implying permission to engage in those same activities. This directly conflicts with the intention of the Non-Competition agreement detailed in the 'Contradicted Text' section, and creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each of Stockholders severally agrees that such Stockholder shall not, at any time following the effectiveness of the Merger, print, publish, divulge or communicate to any person, firm, corporation or other business organization or use for his or their own account any business contacts, customers, suppliers, technology, product designs and specifications, know-how, trade secrets, marketing techniques, promotional materials or other secret or confidential information relating to the Business or any other businesses then being engaged in by AUGI or any of its subsidiaries, or any secret or confidential information relating to the affairs, dealings and concerns of the Company, AUGI or any of the subsidiaries of AUGI (collectively, the "Confidential Information") which the Stockholder has received or obtained while an employee or member of the Board of Directors of AUGI or the Company (whether or not developed, devised, or otherwise created in whole or in part by the efforts of the Stockholder).
perturbation.changed_text: Each of Stockholders severally agrees that such Stockholder may, at any time following the effectiveness of the Merger, print, publish, divulge or communicate to any person, firm, corporation or other business organization or use for his or their own account any business contacts, customers, suppliers, technology, product designs and specifications, know-how, trade secrets, marketing techniques, promotional materials or other secret or confidential information relating to the Business or any other businesses then being engaged in by AUGI or any of its subsidiaries, or any secret or confidential information relating to the affairs, dealings and concerns of the Company, AUGI or any of the subsidiaries of AUGI (collectively, the "Confidential Information") which the Stockholder has received or obtained while an employee or member of the Board of Directors of AUGI or the Company (whether or not developed, devised, or otherwise created in whole or in part by the efforts of the Stockholder).
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall not' to 'may', the clause contradicts its purpose and definition of Confidential Information. Initially intended to prevent disclosure, the change now implies the Stockholders are permitted to disclose Confidential Information. This directly opposes the very idea of keeping the information confidential, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information. (a) Each of Stockholders severally agrees that such Stockholder may, at any time following the effectiveness of the Merger, print, publish, divulge or communicate to any person, firm, corporation or other business organization or use for his or their own account any business contacts, customers, suppliers, technology, product designs and specifications, know-how, trade secrets, marketing techniques, promotional materials or other secret or confidential information relating to the Business or any other businesses then being engaged in by AUGI or any of its subsidiaries, or any secret or confidential information relating to the affairs, dealings and concerns of the Company, AUGI or any of the subsidiaries of AUGI (collectively, the "Confidential Information") which the Stockholder has received or obtained while an employee or member of the Board of Directors of AUGI or the Company (whether or not developed, devised, or otherwise created in whole or in part by the efforts of the Stockholder). The term "Confidential Information" does not include information which information and its relevance in the particular instance (A) is a matter of common knowledge or public record, (B) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of disclosure by the Stockholder, (C) which information and its relevance is generally known in Business, (D) the Stockholder can demonstrate that such information and its relevance in the applicable instance, was already known to the recipient thereof other than by reason of any breach of any obligation of the Stockholder under this Agreement or any other confidentiality or non-disclosure agreement, or (E) the information is required to be disclosed by law or pursuant to court order or subpoeona.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'shall not' to 'may', directly contradicting the original intent of the clause to prevent stockholders from disclosing confidential information. This creates a direct conflict with the definition of 'Confidential Information' in the contradicted text, which aims to protect this information. By allowing disclosure, the changed text undermines the purpose of keeping the information confidential, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The original text clearly prohibits certain actions related to Confidential Information, and the changed text now permits those exact same actions. This satisfies the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: The term "Confidential Information" does not include information which information and its relevance in the particular instance (A) is a matter of common knowledge or public record, (B) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of disclosure by the Stockholder, (C) which information and its relevance is generally known in Business, (D) the Stockholder can demonstrate that such information and its relevance in the applicable instance, was already known to the recipient thereof other than by reason of any breach of any obligation of the Stockholder under this Agreement or any other confidentiality or non-disclosure agreement, or (E) the information is required to be disclosed by law or pursuant to court order or subpoeona.
perturbation.changed_text: The term "Confidential Information" does include information which information and its relevance in the particular instance (A) is a matter of common knowledge or public record, (B) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of disclosure by the Stockholder, (C) which information and its relevance is generally known in Business, (D) the Stockholder can demonstrate that such information and its relevance in the applicable instance, was already known to the recipient thereof other than by reason of any breach of any obligation of the Stockholder under this Agreement or any other confidentiality or non-disclosure agreement, or (E) the information is required to be disclosed by law or pursuant to court order or subpoeona.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'does not include' to 'does include', the clause now includes public knowledge and legally required disclosures under the definition of Confidential Information. This directly contradicts the exceptions, making them part of what is considered confidential, which causes confusion and contradiction within the text, regarding what should be kept confidential.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term "Confidential Information" does include information which information and its relevance in the particular instance (A) is a matter of common knowledge or public record, (B) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of disclosure by the Stockholder, (C) which information and its relevance is generally known in Business, (D) the Stockholder can demonstrate that such information and its relevance in the applicable instance, was already known to the recipient thereof other than by reason of any breach of any obligation of the Stockholder under this Agreement or any other confidentiality or non-disclosure agreement, or (E) the information is required to be disclosed by law or pursuant to court order or subpoeona.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters 'does not include' to 'does include' in the definition of Confidential Information. This means that information that was previously considered outside the scope of confidential information, such as public knowledge, is now included. The 'Contradicted Text' is the same as the 'Changed Text', which makes the contradiction directly within the text. The change creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict, making the definition contradictory.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary, express or implied, contained in this Agreement, the Merger Agreement or in any other "Transaction Document" (as that term is defined in the Merger Agreement), no future act of omission or commission by AUGI or any of its affiliates, or other actual or alleged breach by AUGI or such affiliates of any of their covenants or agreements contained in any Transaction Documents (in addition to this Agreement), shall in any way be deemed to modify, limit or terminate the covenants and agreements of the Stockholders contained in Section 1 and Section 2 hereof, all of which shall remain in full force and effect in accordance with their respective terms.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary, express or implied, contained in this Agreement, the Merger Agreement or in any other "Transaction Document" (as that term is defined in the Merger Agreement), any future act of omission or commission by AUGI or any of its affiliates, or other actual or alleged breach by AUGI or such affiliates of any of their covenants or agreements contained in any Transaction Documents (in addition to this Agreement), shall in any way be deemed to modify, limit or terminate the covenants and agreements of the Stockholders contained in Section 1 and Section 2 hereof, all of which shall remain in full force and effect in accordance with their respective terms.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'no future act' to 'any future act', the clause now suggests that any breach by AUGI *can* modify or terminate the Stockholders' covenants. This is an in-text contradiction, as it directly opposes the original intent of the clause to ensure the Stockholders' obligations remain regardless of AUGI's actions. This contradiction creates uncertainty about the conditions under which the non-compete and non-disclosure agreements can be nullified.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary, express or implied, contained in this Agreement, the Merger Agreement or in any other "Transaction Document" (as that term is defined in the Merger Agreement), any future act of omission or commission by AUGI or any of its affiliates, or other actual or alleged breach by AUGI or such affiliates of any of their covenants or agreements contained in any Transaction Documents (in addition to this Agreement), shall in any way be deemed to modify, limit or terminate the covenants and agreements of the Stockholders contained in Section 1 and Section 2 hereof, all of which shall remain in full force and effect in accordance with their respective terms.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'no future act' to 'any future act' directly contradicts the original intention of the clause. The original text explicitly states that no future actions by AUGI can modify or terminate the stockholders' covenants. The changed text implies that such actions *can* modify or terminate those covenants, creating a direct conflict and making the agreement internally inconsistent and contradictory. The contradicted text is the same, so this shows the impact of the change in creating contradiction in the location.

perturbation.original_text: Without intending to limit the remedies available to AUGI or the Company, each Stockholder further agrees that damages at law will be an insufficient remedy to AUGI if such Stockholder(s) breaches the terms of this Agreement and that AUGI or the Company may apply for and have injunctive relief in any court of competent jurisdiction to restrain the breach or threatened breach of or otherwise to specifically enforce any of the covenants contained herein, in each case without the necessity of proving damages or posting bond.
perturbation.changed_text: Without intending to limit the remedies available to AUGI or the Company, each Stockholder further agrees that damages at law will be a sufficient remedy to AUGI if such Stockholder(s) breaches the terms of this Agreement and that AUGI or the Company may apply for and have injunctive relief in any court of competent jurisdiction to restrain the breach or threatened breach of or otherwise to specifically enforce any of the covenants contained herein, in each case without the necessity of proving damages or posting bond.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'insufficient remedy' to 'sufficient remedy', the clause now says that financial damages alone *are* adequate. This conflicts with the part of the sentence arguing that injunctive relief can be applyied without the necessity of proving damages, which makes a contradiction because, injunctive relief requires that financial relief is not sufficient. This contradiction creates uncertainty about the recourse that AUGI has against the Stockholders, and the standard of proof they must meet.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Without intending to limit the remedies available to AUGI or the Company, each Stockholder further agrees that damages at law will be a sufficient remedy to AUGI if such Stockholder(s) breaches the terms of this Agreement and that AUGI or the Company may apply for and have injunctive relief in any court of competent jurisdiction to restrain the breach or threatened breach of or otherwise to specifically enforce any of the covenants contained herein, in each case without the necessity of proving damages or posting bond.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies "insufficient remedy" to "sufficient remedy". The Perturbation Explanation states that this change creates a contradiction because it conflicts with the part of the sentence that allows injunctive relief without proving damages (because financial relief is supposedly sufficient). The Contradicted Text is the Changed Text. This adheres to the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, because the clause is placed with conflicting implications. This creates uncertainty about the obligations and rights of each party.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall become effective upon the effectiveness of the Merger and shall terminate prior to the expiration of the Restrictive Period only with the written consent of AUGI.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall not become effective upon the effectiveness of the Merger and shall terminate prior to the expiration of the Restrictive Period only with the written consent of AUGI.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall become' to 'shall not become' the clause now directly says that the agreement will not go into effect, but follows the phrase 'shall terminate', making this in text contradiction. It directly opposes the nature of the agreement, indicating the agreement is not effective, but at the same time, says that it shall terminate, implying that it did became effective.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall not become effective upon the effectiveness of the Merger and shall terminate prior to the expiration of the Restrictive Period only with the written consent of AUGI.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'shall become' to 'shall not become' directly contradicts the nature of an agreement taking effect. The altered text states the agreement will not become effective upon the merger but also mentions it shall terminate, implying it did become effective. The contradicted text is the changed text, which creates a structural flaw in the agreement because the clause implies conflicting implications by stating that the agreement does not become effective but can terminate, suggesting that it did come into effect, aligning with the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.
